# econark notation registry вҖ” v1.0.0

**AUTO-GENERATED** from `notation.yml` + `terminology.yml`. DO NOT EDIT this file; edit the YAML and run `python gen/gen_markdown.py`. A notational or terminology ruling is a PR against the YAML (see `README.md`).

## Symbols

| Object | Symbol | Macro | Expansion | Python | Gloss |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| assets-next | a_{t+1} | `\aNrmNxt` | `a\Nxt` |  | next-period end-of-period assets (time-pair convention) |
| assets-now | a_t | `\aNrmNow` | `a\Now` |  | current-period end-of-period assets (path-equation dating) |
| bank-balances | b | `\bNrm` | `b` | `bNrm` | beginning-of-period nonhuman wealth: returns realized on last period's assets |
| bank-balances-next | b_{t+1} | `\bNrmNxt` | `\bNrm\Nxt` |  | next-period bank balances (time-pair convention; row added 2026-07-17 вҖ” \bNrmNxt was already in corpus use but missing here) |
| bank-balances-now | b_t | `\bNrmNow` | `\bNrm\Now` |  | current-period bank balances (path-equation dating) |
| buffer-adjustment | J | `\bufAdj` | `J` |  | the (Kimball) adjustment вҖ” the per-period provision moving the buffer stock toward its target; SIGNED in general (an oversized buffer stock adjusts downward), provably positive in the high-wealth asymptotic region (the forcing floor) |
| buffer-adjustment-function | J(В·) upright | `\bufAdjFunc` | `\mathrm{J}` |  | the adjustment as a FUNCTION, upright (applications J(wМ„); the value guise is bare italic J) |
| compensation-exponent | qМғ | `\qtilde` | `\tilde{q}` |  | trial/compensation exponent in the detection machinery |
| constraint-end-exponent | qвҶ“ | `\qLo` | `q{\downarrow}` |  | constraint-end (bottom-knot) approach exponent вҖ” the down-arrow partner of qвҶ‘; PROVEN qвҶ“ = ПҒ |
| consumption-function | c(В·) upright | `\cFunc` | `\mathrm{c}` |  | the consumption FUNCTION, upright guise (the value guise is bare italic c) |
| consumption-next | c_{t+1} | `\cNrmNxt` | `c\Nxt` |  | next-period consumption value (time-pair convention v2: the \*Now/\*Nxt families are defined THROUGH the \Now/\Nxt suffixes, so the dialect flip is the two suffix definitions вҖ” NOT per-macro renewal; the old flip example 'c^{\prime}' is a verified double-superscript trap) |
| consumption-now | c_t | `\cNrmNow` | `c\Now` |  | current-period consumption value (ruling 9 v2: path equations date every variable) |
| crra | ПҒ | `\CRRA` | `\rho` | `CRRA` | coefficient of relative risk aversion |
| gap-remainder-left | О·_L | `\etaL` | `\eta_{L}` |  | left remainder of the exact one-step gap identity (Lemma 5.1); collects evaluation-point/linearization corrections; vanishes in the asymptotic region |
| gap-remainder-right | О·_R | `\etaR` | `\eta_{R}` |  | right remainder of the exact one-step gap identity (Lemma 5.1) |
| growth-factor | О“ | `\PermGroFac` | `\Gamma` | `PermGroFac` | permanent-income growth factor |
| growth-rate | g | `\PermGroRte` | `g` |  | log permanent-income growth rate |
| guise-convention |  |  |  |  | ruling 9 v2 (owner-blessed 2026-07-17): a Latin letter naming both a quantity and a function is ITALIC as a quantity (bare letter = the *Nrm object) and UPRIGHT as a function (the *Func object), always with an argument; Greek-letter functions stay italic (KaTeX has no upright lowercase Greek) вҖ” arguments disambiguate; primes are reserved for differentiation and appear only on upright letters (time never appears as a prime); path equations date every variable, function equations keep the state bare and date the function |
| high-wealth-exponent | qвҶ‘ | `\qHi` | `q{\uparrow}` |  | high-wealth decay-exponent characteristic root (random-multiplicative eigenvalue); realized exponent min(1, qвҶ‘) |
| human-wealth | h | `\hNrm` | `h` | `hNrm` | normalized human wealth, current period's income INCLUDED (BST eq-HDef) |
| human-wealth-optimist | hМ„ | `\hNrmOpt` | `\bar{h}` |  | optimist's human wealth (bar = optimist) |
| human-wealth-pessimist | hМІ | `\hNrmPes` | `\underline{h}` |  | pessimist's (minimal) human wealth |
| log-excess-resources | Ој | `\logmNrmEx` | `\boldsymbol{\mu}` |  | log excess resources |
| log-growth-patience-rate | Гҫ_g | `\GPRte` | `\thorn_{\PermGroRte}` |  | log growth-patience rate вҖ” lowercase-thorn partner of Гһ_О“; NEGATIVE under GIC; the per-period PF descent rate of ln wМ„ is вҲ’Гҫ_g |
| mpc-max | ОәМ„ | `\MPCmax` | `\overline{\kappa}` | `MPCmax` | limiting MPC as mвҶ’0 (worst-atom) |
| mpc-min | ОәМІ | `\MPCmin` | `\underline{\kappa}` | `MPCmin` | limiting MPC as mвҶ’вҲһ (PF MPC); pessimist/lower-bound underline |
| patience-abs | Гһ | `\APFac` | `\text{Гһ}` |  | absolute patience factor |
| patience-growth | Гһ_О“ | `\GPFacRaw` | `\text{Гһ}_{\Gamma}` |  | growth patience factor |
| patience-return | Гһ_R | `\RPFac` | `\text{Гһ}_{R}` |  | return patience factor |
| period-next | t+1 | `\prdNxt` | `t+1` |  | next-period index (house time tooling; = econark-shortcuts \prdNxt) |
| period-next-suffix | _{t+1} | `\Nxt` | `_{\prdNxt}` |  | next-period decoration suffix вҖ” the other half of the flip point: subscript dialect '_{\prdNxt}'; prime dialect the LITERAL apostrophe вҖ” NEVER '^{\prime}' (double superscript on \cNrmNxt^{-\rho} forms; both traps verified against the bundled KaTeX 2026-07-17) |
| period-now-suffix | _t | `\Now` | `_{\prdt}` |  | current-period decoration suffix вҖ” HALF THE DIALECT FLIP POINT (ruling 9 v2): subscript dialect '_{\prdt}'; prime dialect '{}' вҖ” NEVER the empty string (mystmd drops empty macros -> undefined control sequence; verified 2026-07-17) |
| permanent-shock | ПҲ | `\permShk` | `\psi` | `PermShk` | permanent shock, E[ПҲ]=1, bounded support |
| permanent-shock-next | ПҲ_{t+1} | `\permShkNxt` | `\psi\Nxt` |  | next-period permanent shock (time-pair convention) |
| precautionary-saving | x | `\psav` | `x` |  | precautionary saving вҖ” the eXtra saving induced by precaution: the shortfall of consumption below BST's perfect-foresight rule |
| precautionary-saving-function | x(В·) upright | `\psavFunc` | `\mathrm{x}` |  | precautionary saving as a FUNCTION, upright (applications x(m), x(wМ„); the value guise is bare italic x) |
| precautionary-saving-next | x_{t+1} | `\psavNxt` | `\psav\Nxt` |  | next-period precautionary saving (time-pair convention) |
| precautionary-saving-now | x_t | `\psavNow` | `\psav\Now` |  | current-period precautionary saving (path-equation dating) |
| pseudo-target | mМҢ | `\mBalLvl` | `\check{m}` | `mBalLvl` | balanced-growth pseudo-target (GIC-Raw) |
| resources-excess | mМҲ | `\mNrmEx` | `\ddot{m}` |  | excess market resources above the natural borrowing constraint |
| resources-next | m_{t+1} | `\mNrmNxt` | `m\Nxt` |  | next-period market resources (time-pair convention) |
| resources-now | m_t | `\mNrmNow` | `m\Now` |  | current-period market resources (path-equation dating) |
| return-growth-ratio | в„ӣ | `\RNrmByG` | `\mathcal{R}` |  | growth-normalized return |
| return-region | О¶ (edge) |  |  |  | the compact interval around the target that the renewal argument uses; exists because Оё has bounded support; upper edge computable |
| target | mМӮ | `\mTrgNrm` | `\hat{m}` | `mTrgNrm` | individual buffer-stock target: E[mвҖІ/m] = 1 at mМӮ (WRIC+FVAC+GIC-Mod) |
| thorn-glyph | Гҫ | `\thorn` | `\text{Гҫ}` |  | lowercase thorn glyph provision вҖ” the RATE partner of Гһ; referenced by \GPRte/\APRte/\RPRte in econark-shortcuts.sty, which never defined it (latent-broken until this ruling) |
| transitory-shock-all | Оҫ | `\tranShkAll` | `\xi` |  | transitory shock INCLUDING the zero-income atom |
| transitory-shock-next | Оё_{t+1} | `\tranShkNxt` | `\theta\Nxt` |  | next-period transitory shock (time-pair convention; powerlaw-pages Оё dialect) |
| travel-time-to-target | П„ | `\trvTime` | `\tau` |  | periods of perfect-foresight descent from wМ„ to the target neighborhood (the log-clock); frees T to mean ONLY the terminal/resumption date |
| utility-function | u(В·) upright | `\uFunc` | `\mathrm{u}` |  | the utility function (always function guise; u' etc. are derivatives) |
| wealth-total-pf | wМ„ | `\wbar` | `\bar{w}` | `wBarNrm` | perfect-foresight total wealth (human and market) вҖ” the optimist's wealth, viewed after returns realize on last period's kapital |
| wealth-total-pf-next | wМ„_{t+1} | `\wbarNxt` | `\wbar\Nxt` |  | next-period perfect-foresight total wealth (time-pair convention) |
| wealth-total-pf-now | wМ„_t | `\wbarNow` | `\wbar\Now` |  | current-period perfect-foresight total wealth (path-equation dating) |
| zero-income-prob | в„ҳ | `\pZero` | `\wp` | `pZero` | probability of the zero-income event (the worst transitory atom) |

## Definitions, dialects & rulings

### bank-balances

- **define:** b = в„ӣa = m вҲ’ 1 (nonstochastic в„ӣ; stochastic form bвҖІ = (R/(О“ПҲвҖІ))a)
- **ruling:** 2026-07-14 В· source: BST feedback N2 (BST's own b := mвҲ’1 overbrace, BufferStockTheory.md MPCminDefn block)

### buffer-adjustment

- **define:** wМ„В·J(wМ„) вҶ’ ОәМІ(ПҒ+1)ПғВІ/(2Гһ_О“)   (transitory-only limit)
- **alias:** RETIRED same-PR before any consumption: \KimPrem / 'Kimball precautionary premium' / 'dividend' вҖ” banned terms (terminology.yml): they connote a strictly positive flow
- **conflicts checked:** J carries only generic letter macros (\JLvl/\JNrm/\JFunc) in econark-shortcuts.sty вҖ” no semantic claim (verified 2026-07-16)
- **ruling:** 2026-07-17 В· source: owner rulings 2026-07-16 (the flow is 'the adjustment') as CORRECTED 2026-07-17: the buffer stock is END-OF-PERIOD ASSETS a (a LEVEL, not a wealth-cushion deviation; target Гў = mМӮ вҲ’ c(mМӮ), в„ӣГў + 1 = mМӮ at ПҲвүЎ1); 'financing' retired as x's narrative name вҖ” x is the discounted stock of adjustments still to be provided

### buffer-adjustment-function

- **conflicts checked:** \JFunc in econark-shortcuts is the generic letter macro вҖ” no semantic clash

### compensation-exponent

- **alias:** alt_proof_compactified.md retains s for this internally (declared bridge)
- **ruling:** 2026-07-14 В· source: feedback revision: frees the letter s for precautionary saving on reader-facing pages

### constraint-end-exponent

- **define:** MPC deficit ОәМ„ вҲ’ c/mбөү вүҚ (mбөү)^ПҒ; in MoM coords ПҮ(Ој) = Ој + bвӮҖ + O(e^{ПҒОј})
- **alias:** supersedes qВ° (\qcirc, q_{\circ}) вҖ” the 2026-07-13 constraint-end theorem symbol
- **ruling:** 2026-07-14 В· source: owner ruling (supersedes qВ° = \qcirc, 2026-07-13 constraint-end theorem): arrow decorations В· rationale: PROVEN qвҶ“ = ПҒ (CRRA); the letter r stays reserved for the interest rate

Since econark-notation.sty uses \providecommand, a doc that also loads econark-shortcuts.sty gets \mathcal{G} (shortcuts wins); load econark-notation alone for the base dialect. ### high-wealth-exponent

- **define:** root of E[ПҲ^{1+q}] = в„ӣВ·Гһ_О“^q  (ПҲвүЎ1: qвҶ‘ = ln в„ӣ / ln(1/Гһ_О“))

### human-wealth

- **define:** h_t = (1вҲ’в„ӣ^{вҲ’(TвҲ’t+1)})/(1вҲ’в„ӣвҒ»В№);  limit h = 1/(1вҲ’в„ӣвҒ»В№) = R/(RвҲ’О“)
- **dialect `hark-python`:** expansion `h вҲ’ 1`; home: HARK's hNrm EXCLUDES the current period; **MIGRATION-PENDING (ruling 4): a SEMANTIC divergence вҖ” needs an explicit owner ruling (rename / re-derive / waiver) before any HARK code change; do not touch code from this mission**; verified in HARK source 2026-07-14
- **ruling:** 2026-07-14 В· source: BST feedback N1 В· rationale: one h, BST's; the shadow h_BST and local future-only h are banned

- **define:** Ој = ln mМҲ

- **define:** Гҫ_g := ln Гһ_О“   (so вҲ’Гҫ_g = ln(1/Гһ_О“) > 0 under GIC; ПҲвүЎ1: qвҶ‘ = ln в„ӣ/(вҲ’Гҫ_g))

- **ruling:** 2026-07-16 В· source: owner ruling Q2 (reactions round): option (i), sign = ln of the factor; use the existing macro

- **define:** x(m) := cМ„(m) вҲ’ c(m) = ОәМІВ·wМ„ вҲ’ c(m), written x(wМ„) in the perfect-foresight-wealth coordinate

- **ruling:** 2026-07-16 В· source: owner ruling Q4 (reactions round, verbatim): 'x replaces both, and is defined as eXtra saving (induced by precaution) throughout'; supersedes the 2026-07-14 \pcsNrm/'s' ruling вҖ” MAJOR bump

## Terminology

### Banned terms

| Term | Use instead | Scope | Ruling |
|---|---|---|---|
| dividend | the adjustment (the per-period provision moving the buffer stock toward its target) | powerlaw corpus + buffer-stock expositions | 2026-07-16 (owner (reactions round 2): connotes a strictly positive flow; an oversized buffer stock adjusts DOWNWARD) |
| precautionary premium | the (Kimball) adjustment | powerlaw corpus + buffer-stock expositions | 2026-07-16 (owner (reactions round 2): same sign-connotation objection; credit Kimball, not ArrowвҖ“Pratt, for the precautionary object) |
| precautionary debt | the buffer stock (the wealth cushion vs the target mМӮ); x = its financing | powerlaw corpus | 2026-07-16 (owner (reactions round 2 + Q-A(ii))) |
| production | estimated / estimation | software sense only, in the powerlaw/theory thread's exposition + codebase; economic production functions exempt; committed run logs change only via real re-runs | 2026-07-14 (R5) |
| certainty equivalent | perfect foresight | all prose | 2026-07-14 (T1) |
| target band | 'the target' / 'the return region around the target mМӮ' | all prose | 2026-07-14 (T3) |

### Defined terms

- **GIC-cap** вҖ” ОІ capped a hair below the raw growth-impatience (GIC) boundary; Strong Growth Impatience (GIC-Mod) already fails there вҖ” no target exists; at the estimated ceiling calibration even the mean log-drift is positive _(ruling 2026-07-14, T4 + verification blocker fix)_

### Conventions

- lemmas cited as L0, L1, вҖҰ; primes mark strengthened variants _(ruling 2026-07-14, C6)_
- в„ҳ > 0 is phrased as part of the maintained income-process assumption where load-bearing (WRIC's form, the natural borrowing constraint, ОәМ„); results that survive в„ҳ = 0 must say so explicitly _(ruling 2026-07-14, T5)_
- bar = optimist / upper bound; underline = pessimist / lower bound (BST's cМ„, ОәМІ; MoM's hМ„/hМІ, cМ„/cМІ; wМ„) _(ruling 2026-07-14, N3)_
